front: Painting: paralyzed woman in pink dress crawling through grass toward a farmhouse in Cushing, Maine
Painting: wife Betsy James (30 years younger) modeled the upper body for this painting of a woman with Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease
Painting: the artist was buried at this painting's real-life setting; he met its subject while picking blueberries
Painting: Wind from the Sea depicts fluttering curtains in this painting's subject's house, where the artist had his studio
Painting: after its subject and her brother Alvaro died, the artist painted brown and blue doors representing them
Painting: inspired Terence Malick's Days of Heaven
Painting: reimagined by the collective Kennardphillipps for a 2015 Greenpeace oil spill campaign
Painting: 247 secret portraits of a German neighbor with blonde braids, many nude; purchased by Leonard Andrews
Painting: nude blonde-braided woman on a stool by an open window, illuminated by beams of light
Painting: primarily made in the loft of a millhouse; revealed on covers of both Time ('Stunning Secret') and Newsweek in 1987
Painting: the subject originally worked as a nurse for Karl Kuerner; Michael Palin was the first to interview her
Painting: best-selling catalog was the first art catalog selected by the Book-of-the-Month Club; VHS narrated by Charlton Heston
Painting: boy running down a hill; first painting after the artist's illustrator father was killed at the site
Artist: son of Treasure Island illustrator; father of painter who did Portrait of Pig and pumpkinhead self-portrait
Artist: based in Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania; Brandywine Valley; used Kuerner Farm as frequent setting
Artist: learned egg-tempera technique from brother-in-law Peter Hurd
Artist: Trodden Weed depicts this artist wearing Howard Pyle's shoes after an operation
